root_text.an3.112:1.3: Atīte, bhikkhave, chandarāgaṭṭhāniye dhamme ārabbha chando jāyati;
root_text.an3.112:1.4: anāgate, bhikkhave, chandarāgaṭṭhāniye dhamme ārabbha chando jāyati;
root_text.an3.112:1.5: paccuppanne, bhikkhave, chandarāgaṭṭhāniye dhamme ārabbha chando jāyati.
root_text.an3.112:1.6: Kathañca, bhikkhave, atīte chandarāgaṭṭhāniye dhamme ārabbha chando jāyati?
root_text.an3.112:1.7: Atīte, bhikkhave, chandarāgaṭṭhāniye dhamme ārabbha cetasā anuvitakketi anuvicāreti.
root_text.an3.112:1.8: Tassa atīte chandarāgaṭṭhāniye dhamme ārabbha cetasā anuvitakkayato anuvicārayato chando jāyati.
root_text.an3.112:1.10: Etamahaṁ, bhikkhave, saṁyojanaṁ vadāmi yo cetaso sārāgo.
root_text.an3.112:1.11: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, atīte chandarāgaṭṭhāniye dhamme ārabbha chando jāyati.
root_text.an3.112:2.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, anāgate chandarāgaṭṭhāniye dhamme ārabbha chando jāyati?
root_text.an3.112:2.2: Anāgate, bhikkhave, chandarāgaṭṭhāniye dhamme ārabbha cetasā anuvitakketi anuvicāreti.
root_text.an3.112:2.3: Tassa anāgate chandarāgaṭṭhāniye dhamme ārabbha cetasā anuvitakkayato anuvicārayato chando jāyati.
root_text.an3.112:2.5: Etamahaṁ, bhikkhave, saṁyojanaṁ vadāmi yo cetaso sārāgo.
root_text.an3.112:2.6: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, anāgate chandarāgaṭṭhāniye dhamme ārabbha chando jāyati.
root_text.an3.112:3.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, paccuppanne chandarāgaṭṭhāniye dhamme ārabbha chando jāyati?
root_text.an3.112:3.2: Paccuppanne, bhikkhave, chandarāgaṭṭhāniye dhamme ārabbha cetasā anuvitakketi anuvicāreti.
root_text.an3.112:3.3: Tassa paccuppanne chandarāgaṭṭhāniye dhamme ārabbha cetasā anuvitakkayato anuvicārayato chando jāyati.
root_text.an3.112:3.5: Etamahaṁ, bhikkhave, saṁyojanaṁ vadāmi yo cetaso sārāgo.
root_text.an3.112:3.6: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, paccuppanne chandarāgaṭṭhāniye dhamme ārabbha chando jāyati.
root_text.an3.112:3.7: Imāni kho, bhikkhave, tīṇi nidānāni kammānaṁ samudayāya.
root_text.an3.112:4.3: Atīte, bhikkhave, chandarāgaṭṭhāniye dhamme ārabbha chando na jāyati;
root_text.an3.112:4.4: anāgate, bhikkhave, chandarāgaṭṭhāniye dhamme ārabbha chando na jāyati;
root_text.an3.112:4.5: paccuppanne, bhikkhave, chandarāgaṭṭhāniye dhamme ārabbha chando na jāyati.
root_text.an3.112:5.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, atīte chandarāgaṭṭhāniye dhamme ārabbha chando na jāyati?
root_text.an3.112:5.2: Atītānaṁ, bhikkhave, chandarāgaṭṭhāniyānaṁ dhammānaṁ āyatiṁ vipākaṁ pajānāti.
root_text.an3.112:5.4: Tadabhinivattetvā cetasā abhinivijjhitvā paññāya ativijjha passati.
root_text.an3.112:5.5: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, atīte chandarāgaṭṭhāniye dhamme ārabbha chando na jāyati.
root_text.an3.112:6.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, anāgate chandarāgaṭṭhāniye dhamme ārabbha chando na jāyati?
root_text.an3.112:6.2: Anāgatānaṁ, bhikkhave, chandarāgaṭṭhāniyānaṁ dhammānaṁ āyatiṁ vipākaṁ pajānāti.
root_text.an3.112:6.4: Tadabhinivattetvā cetasā abhinivijjhitvā paññāya ativijjha passati.
root_text.an3.112:6.5: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, anāgate chandarāgaṭṭhāniye dhamme ārabbha chando na jāyati.
root_text.an3.112:7.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, paccuppanne chandarāgaṭṭhāniye dhamme ārabbha chando na jāyati?
root_text.an3.112:7.2: Paccuppannānaṁ, bhikkhave, chandarāgaṭṭhāniyānaṁ dhammānaṁ āyatiṁ vipākaṁ pajānāti, āyatiṁ vipākaṁ viditvā tadabhinivatteti, tadabhinivattetvā cetasā abhinivijjhitvā paññāya ativijjha passati.
root_text.an3.112:7.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, paccuppanne chandarāgaṭṭhāniye dhamme ārabbha chando na jāyati.
root_text.an3.112:7.4: Imāni kho, bhikkhave, tīṇi nidānāni kammānaṁ samudayāyā”ti.

translation_text.an3.112:1.1: “Mendicants, there are these three sources that give rise to deeds.
translation_text.an3.112:1.3: Desire comes up for things that stimulate desire and greed in the past, future, or present.
translation_text.an3.112:1.6: And how does desire come up for things that stimulate desire and greed in the past, future, or present?
translation_text.an3.112:1.7: In your heart you think about and consider things that stimulate desire and greed in the past, future, or present.
translation_text.an3.112:1.8: When you do this, desire comes up,
translation_text.an3.112:1.9: and you get attached to those things.
translation_text.an3.112:1.10: This lust in the heart is what I call a fetter.
translation_text.an3.112:1.11: That’s how desire comes up for things that stimulate desire and greed in the past, future, or present.
translation_text.an3.112:3.7: These are three sources that give rise to deeds.
translation_text.an3.112:4.1: There are these three sources that give rise to deeds.
translation_text.an3.112:4.3: Desire doesn’t come up for things that stimulate desire and greed in the past, future, or present.
translation_text.an3.112:5.1: And how does desire not come up for things that stimulate desire and greed in the past, future, or present?
translation_text.an3.112:5.2: You understand the future result of things that stimulate desire and greed in the past, future, or present.
translation_text.an3.112:5.3: When you know this, you grow disillusioned,
translation_text.an3.112:5.4: your heart becomes dispassionate, and you see it with penetrating wisdom.
translation_text.an3.112:5.5: That’s how desire doesn’t come up for things that stimulate desire and greed in the past, future, or present.
translation_text.an3.112:7.4: These are three sources that give rise to deeds.”

variant_text.an3.112:5.4: Tadabhinivattetvā → tadabhinivajjetvā (bj, sya-all, km); tad abhinivaddhetvā (pts1ed) | abhinivijjhitvā → abhivirājetvā (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed) | ativijjha → abhinivijjha (mr)
